name: Cake Doughnuts (Crocker)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil (2 to 3 inches) in a deep fryer or Dutch oven to 375°F.
2. In a large bowl, beat together 1.5 cups of flour and all remaining ingredients on low speed until well combined, scraping the bowl constantly for about 30 seconds.
3. Beat on medium speed, scraping the bowl occasionally, for an additional 2 minutes.
4. Stir in the remaining flour.
5. Turn dough onto a generously floured cloth-covered board. Roll out to about 1/8 inch thick.
6. Cut into rounds using a floured doughnut cutter.
7. Using a wide spatula, gently slide doughnuts into hot oil.
8. Turn as they rise to the surface.
9. Fry until golden brown, approximately 1 to 1.5 minutes on each side.
10. Remove carefully from the oil without pricking surfaces; drain.
Serve plain or shake one at a time in a bag with powdered sugar. Makes about 2 dozen doughnuts, with approximately 150 calories per doughnut. If using self-rising flour, omit baking powder and salt. For Buttermilk Doughnuts: Do not use self-rising flour. Substitute buttermilk for the milk. Decrease baking powder to 2 teaspoons and add 1 teaspoon baking soda. Note: Hot fat cools somewhat when foods are dropped into it. While frying, the temperature of the fat should be between 370°F and 380°F. If too hot, foods brown before they are cooked through; if too cool, foods become greasy. A deep-fat thermometer is a good investment for maintaining a constant temperature. Do not use a candy thermometer as it does not register high enough.
